REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS N 465 CALIFORNIA ST SF, LLC AND NEWMARK GROUP, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. IT APPEARS FROM THE OPPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CURRENTLY HAVE FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT TRANSFER. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE LEAVE TO FILE A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AT A LATER TIME TO INCLUDE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION WHEN PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENT FACTS (NOT CONCLUSIONS) TO SUPPORT EACH ELEMENT OF THESE CLAIMS. Off Calendar Sep-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS N 465 CALIFORNIA ST SF, LLC AND NEWMARK GROUP, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend. It appears from the opposition that Plaintiff does not currently have facts to support the causes of action for fraudulent transfer. Plaintiff shall have leave to file a motion for leave to amend the complaint at a later time to include these causes of action when Plaintiff has sufficient facts (not conclusions) to support each element of these claims. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/HEK)
